IgBert Unpaired API
===================

IgBert Unpaired is an antibody-specific masked language model (MLM) trained on over two billion unpaired antibody variable region sequences from the Observed Antibody Space (OAS) dataset. Based on a ProtBert architecture, IgBert Unpaired has 420M parameters and employs a 30-layer transformer encoder, supporting single-chain inputs up to 200 residues. The model provides residue-level embeddings suitable for antibody sequence recovery, structure prediction, and property prediction tasks relevant to antibody discovery and engineering.


.. seealso::
   :class: important

   Postman Collection: https://api.biolm.ai/#80869d4d-5ebd-436a-b78f-c281b47342fd

Predict
-------

This endpoint predicts for IgBert Unpaired.

.. tab-set::

    .. tab-item:: Python (biolmai)
        :sync: sdk

        .. code:: python


      from biolmai import Client
      
      client = Client("YOUR_API_KEY")
      
      result = client.predictor(

          model="igbert-unpaired",

          # Add your parameters here

      )

      print(result)

    .. tab-item:: cURL
        :sync: curl

        .. code:: bash

      curl -X POST https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/predictor/ \

        -H 'Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY' \

        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \

        -d '{}'

    .. tab-item:: Python Requests
        :sync: python

        .. code:: python


      import requests
      
      url = "https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/predictor/"

      headers = {

          "Authorization": "Token YOUR_API_KEY",

          "Content-Type": "application/json"

      }

      data = {}
      
      response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=data)

      result = response.json()

      print(result)

    .. tab-item:: R
        :sync: r

        .. code:: r


      library(httr2)

      library(jsonlite)
      
      url <- "https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/predictor/"

      data <- list()
      

      response <- request(url) %>%

        req_headers(

          Authorization = "Token YOUR_API_KEY",

          `Content-Type` = "application/json"

        ) %>%

        req_body_json(data) %>%

        req_perform()
      

      result <- response %>% resp_body_json()

      print(result)

.. http:post:: /api/v3/igbert-unpaired/predict/

   Predict endpoint for IgBert Unpaired.

   :reqheader Content-Type: application/json
   :reqheader Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY

   .. container:: field-heading

      Request JSON Object

   .. container:: field-definition

      - **params** (*object*, optional) --- Configuration parameters:
      
        - **include** (*array of strings*, default: ["mean"]) — Output types to include in response embeddings. Allowed values: "mean", "residue", "logits".
      
      - **items** (*array of objects*, min: 1, max: 32) --- Input antibody sequences:
      
        - **heavy** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 256) — Heavy chain amino acid sequence (unambiguous amino acid codes plus extended characters allowed).
      
        - **light** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 256) — Light chain amino acid sequence (unambiguous amino acid codes plus extended characters allowed).
      
        - **sequence** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 512) — Single unpaired amino acid sequence (unambiguous amino acid codes plus extended characters allowed).
      
        .. note::
          Exactly one of the following must be provided:
          - Both **heavy** and **light** sequences (paired input)
          - **sequence** alone (unpaired input)

   **Example request**:

   .. sourcecode:: http

      POST /api/v3/igbert-unpaired/predict/ HTTP/1.1
      Host: biolm.ai
      Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY
      Content-Type: application/json

      {}

   :statuscode 200: Successful response
   :statuscode 400: Invalid input
   :statuscode 500: Internal server error

   .. container:: field-heading

      Response JSON Object

   .. container:: field-definition

      - **results** (*array of objects*) --- One result per input item, in the order requested:
      
        - **embeddings** (*array of floats*, size: 1024) — Mean embeddings for the sequence
      
        - **residue_embeddings** (*array of arrays of floats*, size: [sequence_length, 1024]) — Per-residue embeddings
      
        - **logits** (*array of arrays of floats*, size: [sequence_length, vocabulary_size]) — Logits for each residue position

   **Example response**:

   .. sourcecode:: http

      HTTP/1.1 200 OK
      Content-Type: application/json

      {
  "results": [
    {
      "log_prob": -11.413089752197266
    },
    {
      "log_prob": -14.090692520141602
    }
  ]
}


Encode
------

This endpoint encodes for IgBert Unpaired.

.. tab-set::

    .. tab-item:: Python (biolmai)
        :sync: sdk

        .. code:: python


      from biolmai import Client
      
      client = Client("YOUR_API_KEY")
      
      result = client.encoder(

          model="igbert-unpaired",

          # Add your parameters here

      )

      print(result)

    .. tab-item:: cURL
        :sync: curl

        .. code:: bash

      curl -X POST https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/encoder/ \

        -H 'Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY' \

        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \

        -d '{}'

    .. tab-item:: Python Requests
        :sync: python

        .. code:: python


      import requests
      
      url = "https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/encoder/"

      headers = {

          "Authorization": "Token YOUR_API_KEY",

          "Content-Type": "application/json"

      }

      data = {}
      
      response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=data)

      result = response.json()

      print(result)

    .. tab-item:: R
        :sync: r

        .. code:: r


      library(httr2)

      library(jsonlite)
      
      url <- "https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/encoder/"

      data <- list()
      

      response <- request(url) %>%

        req_headers(

          Authorization = "Token YOUR_API_KEY",

          `Content-Type` = "application/json"

        ) %>%

        req_body_json(data) %>%

        req_perform()
      

      result <- response %>% resp_body_json()

      print(result)

.. http:post:: /api/v3/igbert-unpaired/encode/

   Encode endpoint for IgBert Unpaired.

   :reqheader Content-Type: application/json
   :reqheader Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY

   .. container:: field-heading

      Request JSON Object

   .. container:: field-definition

      - **params** (*object*, optional) --- Configuration parameters:
      
        - **include** (*array of strings*, default: ["mean"]) — Output types to include in response:
        
          - Allowed values:
          
            - "mean" — Mean embedding representation
            - "residue" — Per-residue embedding representations
            - "logits" — Model logits
      
      - **items** (*array of objects*, min: 1, max: 32) --- Input antibody sequences:
      
        - **heavy** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 256) — Heavy chain amino acid sequence (standard amino acid codes plus extended set)
        - **light** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 256) — Light chain amino acid sequence (standard amino acid codes plus extended set)
        - **sequence** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 512) — Unpaired amino acid sequence (standard amino acid codes plus extended set)
      
        - **Constraints**:
        
          - Provide either both "heavy" and "light" sequences (paired input), or a single "sequence" (unpaired input)
          - Cannot provide both ("heavy", "light") and "sequence" simultaneously

   **Example request**:

   .. sourcecode:: http

      POST /api/v3/igbert-unpaired/encode/ HTTP/1.1
      Host: biolm.ai
      Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY
      Content-Type: application/json

      {}

   :statuscode 200: Successful response
   :statuscode 400: Invalid input
   :statuscode 500: Internal server error

   .. container:: field-heading

      Response JSON Object

   .. container:: field-definition

      - **results** (*array of objects*) --- One result per input item, in the order requested:
      
        - **embeddings** (*array of floats*, size: 1024) — Mean protein embeddings
        - **residue_embeddings** (*array of arrays of floats*, size: [variable_length, 1024]) — Per-residue embeddings
        - **logits** (*array of arrays of floats*, size: [variable_length, 20]) — Logits for each amino acid position

Generate
--------

This endpoint gensats for IgBert Unpaired.

.. tab-set::

    .. tab-item:: Python (biolmai)
        :sync: sdk

        .. code:: python


      from biolmai import Client
      
      client = Client("YOUR_API_KEY")
      
      result = client.generator(

          model="igbert-unpaired",

          # Add your parameters here

      )

      print(result)

    .. tab-item:: cURL
        :sync: curl

        .. code:: bash

      curl -X POST https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/generator/ \

        -H 'Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY' \

        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \

        -d '{}'

    .. tab-item:: Python Requests
        :sync: python

        .. code:: python


      import requests
      
      url = "https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/generator/"

      headers = {

          "Authorization": "Token YOUR_API_KEY",

          "Content-Type": "application/json"

      }

      data = {}
      
      response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=data)

      result = response.json()

      print(result)

    .. tab-item:: R
        :sync: r

        .. code:: r


      library(httr2)

      library(jsonlite)
      
      url <- "https://biolm.ai/api/v3/igbert-unpaired/generator/"

      data <- list()
      

      response <- request(url) %>%

        req_headers(

          Authorization = "Token YOUR_API_KEY",

          `Content-Type` = "application/json"

        ) %>%

        req_body_json(data) %>%

        req_perform()
      

      result <- response %>% resp_body_json()

      print(result)

.. http:post:: /api/v3/igbert-unpaired/generate/

   Generate endpoint for IgBert Unpaired.

   :reqheader Content-Type: application/json
   :reqheader Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY

   .. container:: field-heading

      Request JSON Object

   .. container:: field-definition

      - **params** (*object*, optional) --- Configuration parameters:
      
        - **include** (*array of strings*, default: ["mean"]) — Embedding types to include in the response. Allowed values:
        
          - "mean" — Mean embedding representation
          - "residue" — Per-residue embeddings
          - "logits" — Raw logits from the model
      
      - **items** (*array of objects*, min: 1, max: 32) --- Input sequences for generation:
      
        - **heavy** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 256) — Heavy chain amino acid sequence containing standard amino acids and at least one "*" placeholder for generation
        - **light** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 256) — Light chain amino acid sequence containing standard amino acids and at least one "*" placeholder for generation
        - **sequence** (*string*, optional, min length: 1, max length: 512) — Single-chain amino acid sequence containing standard amino acids and at least one "*" placeholder for generation

   **Example request**:

   .. sourcecode:: http

      POST /api/v3/igbert-unpaired/generate/ HTTP/1.1
      Host: biolm.ai
      Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY
      Content-Type: application/json

      {}

   :statuscode 200: Successful response
   :statuscode 400: Invalid input
   :statuscode 500: Internal server error

   .. container:: field-heading

      Response JSON Object

   .. container:: field-definition

      - **results** (*array of objects*) --- One result per input item, in the order requested:
      
        - **embeddings** (*array of floats*, size: 1024) — Mean embeddings for the sequence
        - **residue_embeddings** (*array of arrays of floats*, shape: [sequence_length, 1024]) — Per-residue embeddings
        - **logits** (*array of arrays of floats*, shape: [sequence_length, vocabulary_size]) — Logits for each residue position
      
      - **results** (*array of objects*) --- One result per input item, in the order requested:
      
        - **heavy** (*string*, optional) — Generated heavy chain sequence
        - **light** (*string*, optional) — Generated light chain sequence
        - **sequence** (*string*, optional) — Generated unpaired sequence
      
      - **results** (*array of objects*) --- One result per input item, in the order requested:
      
        - **log_prob** (*float*) — Log probability of the sequence

   **Example response**:

   .. sourcecode:: http

      HTTP/1.1 200 OK
      Content-Type: application/json

      {
  "results": [
    {
      "sequence": "ACDEFGHIKLLNPQRSTVWY"
    },
    {
      "sequence": "MKTIIALSYILCLVFADYKDDDDK"
    }
  ]
}


Performance
-----------

- IgBert Unpaired runs inference on NVIDIA T4 GPUs, providing GPU-accelerated performance optimized specifically for antibody sequence embedding tasks.
- Typical inference completion times are approximately 2-4 seconds per batch of up to 32 unpaired sequences, depending on sequence length.
- IgBert Unpaired offers significantly faster inference speeds compared to IgT5 Unpaired due to its smaller model size (420M parameters vs. 3B parameters), resulting in approximately 3-5x faster embedding generation.
- Compared to general protein language models (e.g., ProtBert), IgBert Unpaired achieves substantially higher accuracy in antibody-specific sequence recovery tasks, particularly in hypervariable CDR loops (e.g., 90.43% vs. 65.60% accuracy for CDRH1).
- IgBert Unpaired embeddings perform better than general protein models (ProtBert) on antibody binding affinity prediction tasks, with an R² improvement of approximately 0.08-0.18 across standard antibody binding datasets (e.g., Shanehsazzadeh et al., Warszawski et al., Koenig et al.).
- Due to its antibody-specific training, IgBert Unpaired achieves lower pseudo-perplexity scores (1.031 total VH/VL) compared to general protein models (ProtBert: 1.326 total VH/VL), indicating superior modeling of antibody sequence distributions.
- IgBert Unpaired embeddings perform slightly worse than paired IgBert embeddings on binding affinity prediction tasks (R² difference of ~0.02-0.03), as paired models benefit from cross-chain information unavailable to unpaired models.
- BioLM has optimized IgBert Unpaired deployment through distributed inference strategies, efficient memory management, and GPU-specific optimizations, ensuring scalable and cost-effective inference performance.


Applications
------------

- Antibody sequence recovery for therapeutic design and optimization, enabling researchers to accurately reconstruct missing or incomplete antibody sequences, which is crucial for developing effective biotherapeutics and improving existing antibody therapies.

- Binding affinity prediction for antibody-antigen interactions, allowing biotech companies to evaluate and rank potential therapeutic candidates based on their predicted binding strengths, thus streamlining the drug discovery process and reducing experimental costs.

- Expression level prediction for engineered antibodies, assisting in the identification of antibody variants with optimal expression profiles, which is essential for efficient production and scale-up in pharmaceutical manufacturing.

- Cross-chain feature learning for improved antibody design, leveraging paired heavy and light chain data to enhance the understanding of inter-chain interactions, which is critical for designing antibodies with improved specificity and efficacy.

- Sequence recovery in complementarity-determining regions (CDRs) for antibody engineering, focusing on the most variable and functionally important parts of the antibody, thereby facilitating the design of antibodies with enhanced antigen recognition capabilities.


Limitations
-----------

- **Maximum Sequence Length**: The ``heavy`` and ``light`` sequences each have a maximum length of ``256`` amino acids, while the ``sequence`` field for unpaired chains supports up to ``512`` amino acids. Longer antibody sequences must be truncated or split into smaller segments.
- **Batch Size**: The maximum number of sequences per request (``batch_size``) is ``32``. For larger datasets, you must batch your requests accordingly.
- **Model Type (Paired vs Unpaired)**: The IgBert Unpaired model does not leverage paired heavy-light chain information. If your application requires cross-chain context (e.g., predicting binding affinity or stability), consider using the paired IgBert model instead.
- **Prediction of Expression Levels**: IgBert embeddings are optimized for antibody-specific tasks such as binding affinity prediction and sequence recovery. However, general protein models (e.g., ProtT5) may outperform IgBert when predicting broader protein properties like expression levels.
- **Generative Tasks**: IgBert is trained as a masked language model (MLM), making it suitable for sequence encoding and residue-level predictions. It is not optimal for generative antibody design tasks, such as de novo sequence generation or affinity maturation, where autoregressive or encoder-decoder models may be more appropriate.


How We Use It
-------------

The IgBert Unpaired API enables rapid evaluation and optimization of antibody sequences by providing contextualized embeddings trained on extensive antibody sequence data, accelerating antibody design and selection processes. Integrated into BioLM's comprehensive protein engineering workflows, IgBert Unpaired embeddings facilitate accurate predictions of antibody-antigen binding affinity and specificity, streamlining candidate prioritization and reducing the number of design-test cycles required for antibody maturation. Practical applications include:

- Integration with downstream predictive models to rapidly screen candidate antibodies for therapeutic potential.
- Efficient filtering and ranking of antibody variants based on biophysical and functional properties, reducing lab experimentation costs.


Related
-------

- ``IgBert Paired`` – Extends IgBert Unpaired by incorporating paired heavy and light chain data, enabling improved predictions of antibody properties through cross-chain context.
- ``IgT5 Unpaired`` – Complements IgBert Unpaired by using a T5 architecture, providing alternative embedding strategies for antibody sequences and downstream tasks.
- ``ABodyBuilder3 Language`` – Uses language model embeddings like IgBert Unpaired to enhance antibody structure prediction, making them complementary tools for antibody engineering workflows.
- ``AbLang-2`` – Similar BERT-based antibody language model, useful for comparative analysis or ensemble predictions with IgBert Unpaired to improve robustness and accuracy.
